title: Benalmadena Dolphin Watching Boat Rental: Costs, Tips & Best Boats
html:
Benalmadena offers exceptional dolphin watching boat rental opportunities from Puerto Marina, with shared tours from around 35 EUR per person. Whether you prefer a rigid inflatable for speed or a motor yacht for comfort, you can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to find the perfect vessel.

Where to set off in Benalmadena

Most dolphin watching excursions depart from Puerto Deportivo de Benalmadena, also known as Puerto Marina, one of the largest marinas on the Costa del Sol. This modern harbour sits just west of Malaga and offers easy access to the Mediterranean.

Other departure points include the nearby Puerto de Fuengirola and the smaller Cala de Mijas. Many boat rentals also pick up from the bay of Benalmadena itself, close to the Playa de La Carihuela. The stretch of water between the Cabo de Calaburras and Cabo de Mijas is a favoured zone for spotting bottlenose and common dolphins.

Which boat to rent for dolphin watching

Choose a vessel that matches your group size and budget. BoatHire24 lists options from speedboats to luxury catamarans.

  1. Rigid inflatable boat, best for speed and getting close to dolphins; 6, 8 guests; from 250 EUR half-day.
  2. Motor yacht, comfortable and steady; 8, 12 guests; from 500 EUR half-day. This motor yacht includes a fully equipped galley and sun deck.
  3. Sailing catamaran, stable and spacious; 10, 16 guests; from 700 EUR full-day.
  4. Small open boat, ideal for small groups or private trips; 4, 6 guests; from 200 EUR half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Benalmadena?

Prices vary widely but shared tours start at 35 EUR per person and private motorboats from 200 EUR half-day.

  • Shared dolphin tour (2 hours): 35, 50 EUR per adult.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 200, 350 EUR for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 700, 1,200 EUR for up to 16 guests.
  • Luxury motor yacht with crew (full-day): from 1,500 EUR for up to 12 guests.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Benalmadena?

No, you do not need a licence for smaller boats or crewed charters, provided the operator supplies a skipper.

For self-drive rentals under 15 metres, a Spanish recreational boating licence or an ICC with CEVNI inland endorsement is required. Always ask the operator to confirm exact requirements before booking. BoatHire24 listings specify whether a skipper is included.

Local tip: The best dolphin sightings occur in the morning calm before the Levante wind picks up around midday. In August, depart by 9am for the most active pods.

When to go dolphin watching in Benalmadena

The peak season runs from June to September, when seas are warm and dolphin activity is high.

During these months, the Mediterranean off the Costa del Sol is home to resident pods of common and striped dolphins. The Levante wind can create choppy conditions in the afternoon, so morning trips are recommended. Spring and autumn also offer good sightings with fewer crowds.

At a glance

  • Price band: 35 EUR shared, 1,500 EUR private luxury
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Maximum capacity: 2, 16 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Benalmadena

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Benalmadena?

Yes, for small boats under 15 metres you may self-drive with the appropriate licence, but most dolphin watching trips include a skipper.

How far offshore do the dolphins usually appear?

Dolphins are often spotted within 5 nautical miles of the coast, especially around the Cabo de Calaburras area.

What is the cancellation policy for boat rentals?

Policies vary by operator on BoatHire24, so check the listing terms before booking; many offer free cancellation up to 24 hours in advance.

Are life jackets provided for children?

All rental boats must carry adequate safety equipment, including life jackets for children; confirm the age-specific sizes when booking.
